pricingTable.title: Pricing by plan

pricingTable.footnote: Day, week, and month rates may vary by season. Message us on WhatsApp for an exact quote and promotions.

freePlan.title: Free Plan — Café

faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know before booking your spot in downtown Oaxaca.

wifi.question: How fast is the internet?
wifi.answer: We have 1000 Mbps fiber optic in the upstairs coworking area — enough for video calls, heavy uploads, and demanding remote work.

reservation.question: Do I need to book in advance?
reservation.answer: Not always required, but we recommend booking via WhatsApp during peak season (Guelaguetza, Day of the Dead, December) or if you need a desk with monitor or the meeting room.

hours.question: What are Atzomx opening hours?
hours.answer: Monday to Friday 9:00 am – 6:00 pm. Saturday 10:00 am – 8:00 pm. Sunday: check current hours on WhatsApp before visiting.

day-pass.question: Do you offer day, week, or month passes?
day-pass.answer: Yes. In addition to hourly rates, we offer day, week, and month passes for coworking plans. Message us on WhatsApp with the plan you're interested in for current pricing.

meeting-room.question: How do I book the meeting room?
meeting-room.answer: The room fits 2–6 people and includes a 55" Smart TV, whiteboard, WiFi, and coffee. Book via WhatsApp with date, time, and headcount. From $259 MXN per hour.

cafe-vs-coworking.question: What's the difference between the café and coworking?
cafe-vs-coworking.answer: Downstairs (café): work for free while paying only for food and drinks. Upstairs: dedicated coworking with ergonomic desks, A/C, unlimited coffee, and 1000 Mbps fiber — requires a coworking plan.

payment.question: What payment methods do you accept?
payment.answer: We accept cash, credit cards, and debit cards (Visa, Mastercard, American Express).

parking.question: Is there parking nearby?
parking.answer: We're at Los Libres 800-B in downtown Oaxaca, walking distance from the Zócalo. Street parking and private lots are available nearby; many visitors walk or take a taxi/DiDi.

team.title: Built by Oaxacan devs
team.subtitle: We built the space we needed to work remotely from downtown.
team.story1: We're developers who live and work in Oaxaca. For years we bounced between noisy cafés and dim apartments, looking for a place where focus wasn't a luxury.
team.story2: We couldn't find a space that understood Friday deploys, client calls across time zones, or power outlets that weren't three metres away. So we built one.
team.story3: Atzomx came from that shared frustration: a coworking café designed by Oaxacan devs, for anyone working remotely from downtown. We still run it the way we'd want to use it — because we're users too.

footer.payments: All major methods of payment accepted
